breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome's Moon Prediction Proves Our Sun Prediction False: Selective Geometry
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The dome's own predictions page (W021) explicitly predicts >2% angular diameter variation for the moon from local geometry. But the dome makes no parallel prediction for the sun — because solar diameter is constant to 0.01%, which would falsify the same local geometry. The dome author applies the geometric argument where it 'works' (moon) and quietly omits it where it fails (sun). See the updated SEC-2.4.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: W021 smoking gun is a new argument not previously on our site. The selective application of the same geometric principle — predicting variation where some variation exists, omitting the prediction where constancy would falsify geometry — is exactly the kind of motivated reasoning we document throughout.
